content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcel PRACTTcEs CoMMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 9-5g14_2329
\916) 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322-0886
September 15, 2010
Richard Tapley, Controller
Tulare Irrigation District
P.O. Box 1920
Tulare, CA93275
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. 4-10-147
Dear Mr. Tapley:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that our aclvice is based solely
on the provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of othei
conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section
1090.
QUESTTON
Do you, as the treasurer and controller for the Tulare Irrigation District (the ..District")
have a conflict of interest that would bar you from participating in gou"--ental decisions by the
District in choosing the financial institution from which ir will .secuie a loan to finance a bond?
CONCLUSION
No. Based on the facts you provided in your request for advice, yollr economic interest
in your retirement plan and investment in Costco stock ihrough Edward ion"r, Visalia Office
does not bar you from participating in these decisions.
FACTS
You are the treasurer and controller for the Tr-rlare h'rigation District. The District will be
making a decision regarding which financial institution will provide it with a loan for a Bond
' The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections ti l0(x) rhrough g l0l4. All statukrry
ref'erences are k) the GovernmentCode. unless <ltherwise indicated. The regulations of the ririr politisal practices
CommissionarecontainedinSections ltlll0through llJ997()fTitle2of thecalittrrniacodeof Regulations. r\ll
regulatory ret'erences are to'Iitle 2. Division 6 of the Califirrnia Code_of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-10-147
Page No. 2
issuing. Among the potential financial institutions is Edward Jones of Tucson. you invest in
Costco stock as part of your retirement plan through an Edward Jones broker in visalia.
You have asked whether your investment in Costco through Edward Jones will bar you,
under the Act, from participating in the decision regarding the District,s loan.
ANALYSIS
Section 8?.1.09 prohibits any public official from making; parricipating in making, or
using his or her official position to influence a governmental dJciiion in whiJh the official has a
financial interest. A public official has a "finar"iul int"r"st" in a govemmental decision, within
the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on.9ne or more of the pubtic official's economic interests. (Section g7103;
Regulation 18700(a).) The commission has adopted an eight-step standardanalysis for deciding
whether an individual has a disqualifying conflici of interest in a given govemmental decision.
Steps One and TIoi Are you a "public official" making or participating in making a
governmental decision?
A "public official"_is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
govemment agency' . .." (Section 82048.) You are a public official by virtue of your appointed
position with the District. You will be asked to participate in the decision iegaraing which
financial institution will carry the loan on the pistrict's bond issue. you will therefore be
making, participating in making, or influencing a governmental decisior. rs4 g"r.r;r;-'
Regulation 18702.)
Step Three: What are your Economic Interests?
The investment firm that acts as a broker for your applicable stock purchases is the
visalia office of Edward Jones. Your relationship with Edward Jones does not create an
economic interest that is cognizable under the Aci. Your investment is in the stock itself and you
do not have an economic interest in Edward Jones.
You have an economic interest in the stock you own in Costco. you own Iess than
$10,000 of this stock. The Act states that a public offi.iot has an economic interest in ..any
business in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more.,, (Section
87103(a); Regulation 18703'1(a)') You have an economic interest in costco as a stockholder.
Your facts do not suggest that you have any other applicable economic interests.
Step Four: Are the economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the governmental
decisions?
Regulation 18704.1 (copy enclosed) states, in pertinent part, that a business entity in
which a public official has an economic interest is clirectly invo-lved in a governmental decision
if the business entity:


File No. A-10-147
Page No. 3
"(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request, or;
(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject of, the proceeding concerning
the decision before the official or the official's agency. A person is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal,
approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
entitlement or, or contract with, the subject person.,,2
Based on your facts, Costco is not directly involved with the decision that the District
will be making. Your economic interest is, therefore, not directly involved in the decision.
Steps Five and Six: Wilt there be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
Mr. Monahan's economic interest?
The financial effect of a governmental decision on an economic interest in a business
entity that is indirectlyinvolved in the governmental decision is set forth in Regulation lg705.l
costco is a Fortune 500 company, and as such, Regulation ls705.l(c)(t; appliJs. This
regulation states, in pertinent part:
"(1) If the business entity is listed in the Fortune 500 or, if not listed in the
Fortune 500, has revenLles that are no less than the revenues of the
business entity that ranks 500th in the Fortune 500 list, the financial effect
of a governmental decision on the business entity is material if it is
reasonably foreseeable that:
(A) The govemmental decision will result in an increase or decrease in the
business entity's gross revenues for a fiscal year of $10,000,000 or more;
or
(B) The governmental decision will result in the business entity incurring
or avoiding additional expenses or reducing or eliminating exiiting
expenses for a fiscal year in the amount of $2,500,000 or more; or
(C) The governmental decision will result in an increase or decrease in the
value of the business entity's assets or liabilities of $10,000,000 or more.,,
Regulation 18705. 1(c)(1) (copy enclosed).
The decision that will be before the District involves choosing a financial institution that
will administer its loan. The decision before you is to determine wtrictr institution will provide
the District with the most favorable agreement. None of the impacts on your economic interest
as listed above will be present as applied to Costco. Your relationship with Edward Jones as a
broker for your stock investments does not impact the investments th;t you carry therein.
"If a public ofllcial's economic interest is not directly involved in a governmental decision, it is indirectly
involved." (Regularion I 870.1(a).)


File No. A-10-147
Page No.4
The business entity is indirectly involved and the effect is presumed not to be material
Additionally, it is not reasonably foreseeable that the District's decision will have the kind of
impacts on costco that are listed in Regulation 1g705.1, above.
Consequently, based on the facts provided, we see no evidence that there would be a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect of the decisions about the bond and
accompanying loan on yollr economic interest.
Steps Seven and Eight: Does this governmental decision come within any exception to the
confl ict-of-interest rules?
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
The steps above revealed that you are not barred from participating in the decision on the
loan and bond. Even if an exception was applicable, and none is uased on-you, facts, none is
necessary.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
((1r,ll., il l-wr-t
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMRjgl
Enclosure
